friend_id.description: Player who sent the request to accept.
friend_id.title: Friend Id

friend_code.description: Add by 6-character friend_code. Either friend_code or player_id is required.
friend_code.title: Friend Code

player_id.description: Add by player_id directly. Useful when the UI already has the ID (e.g. invite from a match).
player_id.title: Player Id

device_id.description: A stable per-device identifier the client generates and persists. Repeat sign-ins from the same device return the same player_id.
device_id.title: Device Id

AuthSuccessResponse.description: Returned by login / anon / guest / refresh / merge.

game_version.description: Server-side display version. Legacy single-game field; per-game versions live in the games table.
game_version.title: Game Version

jwt_token.description: Bearer token. Pass as 'Authorization: Bearer <jwt_token>' on subsequent requests.
jwt_token.title: Jwt Token

linked_providers.description: Names of OAuth providers linked to this account (e.g. ['google', 'steam']).
linked_providers.title: Linked Providers

protocol_version.description: Server-side network protocol version. Legacy single-game field.
protocol_version.title: Protocol Version

rating.description: Per-game rating from the legacy account row. After Phase 1f-Q the per-game rating moves to the game_profiles row; this response field stays for backward compatibility.
rating.title: Rating

refresh_token.description: Use with POST /v1/auth/refresh to rotate the JWT without re-signing in. Single-use; rotates on every refresh.
refresh_token.title: Refresh Token

AuthSuccessResponse.title: AuthSuccessResponse

CancelFriendRequest.description:
POST /v1/friends/cancel body. Withdraws an outgoing
friend request that hasn't been accepted yet.

friend_id.description: Player who received the request being cancelled.
friend_id.title: Friend Id

CancelFriendRequest.title: CancelFriendRequest

ErrorResponse.description:
Standard error envelope for non-2xx responses.

Every handler returns this shape on validation failures,
auth failures, and unexpected exceptions. The OpenAPI doc
references this as the default response for 4xx/5xx.

error_code.description: Stable machine-readable code clients can branch on (e.g. MISSING_PARAMS, INVALID_REFRESH).
error_code.title: Error Code

message.description: Human-readable error message. Not stable; do not branch on this.
message.title: Message

status.description: Always the literal string 'error' for failures.
status.title: Status

ErrorResponse.title: ErrorResponse

FleetStatusResponse.description:
GET /v1/fleet/status response (also returned by warmup).

Status values:
  - 'cold'      — fleet is at DESIRED=0; warmup may scale it up.
  - 'warming'   — fleet is scaling up; not yet ready.
  - 'ready'     — fleet has at least one ACTIVE instance with
                   an IDLE game session slot.
  - 'unavailable' — no fleet configured for this game (the
                   games config row has fleet_id="").

estimated_remaining_sec.description: Remaining seconds before fleet is expected ready. Null when status is 'ready' or 'unavailable'.
estimated_remaining_sec.title: Estimated Remaining Sec

FleetStatusResponse.title: FleetStatusResponse

source.description: Where the warmup was triggered from ('app_startup', 'lobby_toggle', 'manual'). Used for telemetry / debugging.
source.title: Source

FriendInfo.description: One row in the friends list.

presence.description: Null when the friend is offline (no recent presence heartbeat). Populated otherwise.

FriendInfo.title: FriendInfo

FriendPresence.description:
A friend's current presence (from snoringcat-presence).

Absent / null means the friend is offline.

game_id.description: The game the friend is currently in (e.g. 'hopnbop'). Empty if the friend is online but not in any specific game.
game_id.title: Game Id

updated_at.description: Unix seconds the presence was last refreshed.
updated_at.title: Updated At

FriendPresence.title: FriendPresence

FriendRequestInfo.description: A pending friend request (incoming or outgoing).

player_id.description: The other player's ID (sender for incoming, recipient for outgoing).
player_id.title: Player Id

FriendRequestInfo.title: FriendRequestInfo

GameSessionInfo.description: Connection details for a placed game session.

player_session_ids.description: GameLift player session IDs for the matched players. Pass these to the server on connect for validation.
player_session_ids.title: Player Session Ids

server_hostname.description: DNS hostname for the session (e.g. s-{ip}.game.snoringcat.games). Web clients must use the hostname for valid TLS.
server_hostname.title: Server Hostname

server_ip.description: Public IPv4 of the GameLift container.
server_ip.title: Server Ip

server_port.description: ENet UDP port. WebSocket / WebRTC signaling lives at server_port + 1 (TCP, behind nginx).
server_port.title: Server Port

GameSessionInfo.title: GameSessionInfo

LinkAccountRequest.description:
POST /v1/auth/link body. Adds an OAuth identity to the
currently-signed-in (anonymous or guest) account.
LinkAccountRequest.title: LinkAccountRequest

MarkFriendsSeenRequest.description:
POST /v1/friends/seen body. Marks all incoming friend
requests as seen (clears the unread badge).
MarkFriendsSeenRequest.title: MarkFriendsSeenRequest

MatchmakingStatusResponse.description:
GET /v1/matchmaking/status/{ticket_id} response.

`match_state` cycles through: queued → searching →
placing → completed (or cancelled / timed_out / failed).
`session` is null until match_state == "completed".
MatchmakingStatusResponse.title: MatchmakingStatusResponse

consent_accepted_at.description: Unix seconds when the user accepted the legal consent. Stored on the account; used by the account-export endpoint.
consent_accepted_at.title: Consent Accepted At

consent_legal_version.description: Version string of the legal documents the user accepted (e.g. 'v1.2').
consent_legal_version.title: Consent Legal Version

game_id.description: The game the user is signing in from. Bound into the issued JWT so per-game handlers can reject cross-game token reuse. Empty/missing falls back to the platform's DEFAULT_GAME_ID.
game_id.title: Game Id

provider.description: OAuth provider id (steam, epic, google, facebook, apple).
provider.title: Provider

redirect_uri.description: Redirect URI used during browser OAuth flows (google, facebook, apple). Required for those providers; ignored for steam/epic.
redirect_uri.title: Redirect Uri

PartyInfo.description: Common party state returned by create/invite/join/status/etc.

game_id.description: The game this party is bound to. Cross-game invites are rejected server-side.
game_id.title: Game Id

PartyInfo.title: PartyInfo

PartyMember.description:
One row in the party.members or party.invited list when
rendered for the client. Display name is fetched from the
accounts table at response-build time.
PartyMember.title: PartyMember

party.description: Null when the player is not currently in any party (used by /party/status).

PresenceBatchReadRequest.description:
POST /v1/presence/batch body.

Returns presence rows for all supplied player_ids that have
a fresh row. Players with no row (offline) are omitted from
the response, not returned as nulls.

player_ids.description: Up to 100 player IDs to look up. Larger requests are chunked server-side.
player_ids.title: Player Ids

PresenceBatchReadRequest.title: PresenceBatchReadRequest

presence.description: One entry per online player; offline players are omitted.
presence.title: Presence

PresenceHeartbeatRequest.description:
PUT /v1/presence body.

Clients call this every 60-90 seconds while running. The
server stores the row with a TTL, so missing two heartbeats
decays the player to offline.

game_id.description: The game the player is currently in. Required.
game_id.title: Game Id

rich_presence.description: Free-text presence the friends UI may surface (e.g. 'In lobby', 'Playing 1v1').
rich_presence.title: Rich Presence

session_id.description: Optional GameLift game-session ID for friends who can join.
session_id.title: Session Id

PresenceHeartbeatRequest.title: PresenceHeartbeatRequest

game_id.description: Carry the original game_id forward into the rotated JWT.
game_id.title: Game Id

matchmaking_attributes.description: Free-form attributes passed to FlexMatch (level preferences, latency hints, etc.).
matchmaking_attributes.title: Matchmaking Attributes

SuccessResponse.description: Generic 'no body' success used by void endpoints.
SuccessResponse.title: SuccessResponse

VersionResponse.description:
GET /v1/version response.

Unauthenticated. Clients fetch this at app startup to verify
they're protocol-compatible with the platform before
attempting auth.

game_version.description: Legacy GAME_VERSION env var on the backend. Per-game versions live in the games config table.
game_version.title: Game Version

platform_version.description: Display version of the platform backend (semver).
platform_version.title: Platform Version

protocol_version.description: Legacy single-game protocol_version. Per-game protocol_versions are in the games config table.
protocol_version.title: Protocol Version

VersionResponse.title: VersionResponse

bearerAuth.description: JWT issued by /v1/auth/*. Includes player_id and game_id claims.

info.description: Shared backend for Snoring Cat multiplayer titles. URL-versioned (/v1/, /v2/, ...) so older game clients keep working across platform releases. See docs/api-spec.md for the human-readable overview.
info.title: Snoring Cat Platform API
